what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a point/comment) by Tim in the dragnet-development channel, expanding on iconography constraints and philosophy for the DNA design system, especially for radial menus and the V identity component.
observations: The note is a comment on board://item-d6cdf466 via a commented_on link.
It states that radial menus have no room for labels, so icon-only wedges are required.
It cites a hard constraint of a 44pt minimum thumb-size target.
It references DNA brown-circle iconography from MSG2.
It describes the V component as a symbol representing system identity, but technically just an icon inside a shape.
It claims the same component could hold any icon and that event-action colors/animations are set up the same way as the V.
It ties iconography to minimizing text and cognitive expense, using the brain efficiently.
The note uses informal, transcribed speech with incomplete sentences.
standouts: Icon-only radial-menu wedges are a hard constraint due to lack of label space.
DNA brown-circle iconography (MSG2) is the required visual language.
The V identity component is framed as a generic icon-in-shape instance, not a special snowflake.
Iconography is elevated to a core strategy for reducing cognitive load.
conventions: Follows the channel-memory noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, history).
Uses a board:// URI as the item address.
Markdown body begins with a [point] tag and quotes a prior comment before adding commentary.
Records a single filed history transition.
concerns: The text is spoken/transcribed, with incomplete sentences and unclear phrasing (e.g., 'use The useless brain for them').
The 44pt minimum target is asserted without justification or source in this file.
No explicit action items or acceptance criteria are stated.
References MSG2 and item-d6cdf466 without local context.
notes: Tim's note arguing that DNA iconography is central to the design philosophy, that radial menus must be icon-only at thumb size, and that the V identity component is just a reusable icon-in-shape instance.
questions: What is MSG2 and where is the DNA brown-circle iconography defined?
What is the V component precisely (name, file, API)?
What is the content of item-d6cdf466 that this comment responds to?
What are 'event actions' and how do their colors/animations relate to the V component?
What does 'the useless brain' refer to in this design vocabulary?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a point/comment about iconography; the content matches by expanding into design philosophy, constraints, and component pattern.
intention_signals: It tries to establish iconography as a first-class design-system principle, justify the icon-only radial-menu constraint, and demystify the V identity component by showing it is a generic icon-in-shape pattern.
novelty: The framing that the system's identity symbol (V) is not a bespoke identity artifact but an instance of a reusable icon-in-shape component, decoupling brand identity from special implementation.
contribution: Captures the rationale that iconography reduces cognitive expense and text use; records the hard constraint that radial wedges must be icon-only at thumb size; preserves the V component pattern as a configurable icon container rather than a one-off identity element.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development asserting that DNA iconography is central to reducing cognitive load, that radial menus require icon-only wedges at thumb size using brown-circle DNA glyphs, and that the V identity component is a generic icon-in-shape instance.
intention_for_embedding: To reinforce the importance of the DNA icon system and explain why the V component's identity symbol is structurally reusable for any icon.
